authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary legal baseline for educational records, disclosure limits, and learner/faculty privacy controls that directly shape whether consent-sensitive financial actions can proceed.
used_for: FERPA-safe escalation when evidence is incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational OCR interpretation of disclosure and consent handling needed for tuition/aid action traceability and refusal policy.

authoritative_sources.title: 34 CFR Part 668 (Federal Student Aid Program Administration)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary baseline for federal student aid award administration, disbursement timing, reconciliation, and required record controls.

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Student Aid Handbook
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education, Office of Federal Student Aid
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official operational manual for financial-aid processing and servicing workflows that frames evidence expectations and exception contexts.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary guidance on informed-consent and ethics-status changes for study-linked records; critical for escalation and boundary decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance (2 CFR Part 200)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides federal stewardship and documentation requirements for grant-linked costs and financial records.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Program and Enrollment Data
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official education reporting data taxonomy used for aligning learner/program identifiers and institutional context.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Interoperable model for learner/course and enrollment records used to reconcile systems-of-record.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized accreditation governance baseline that defines when financial outcomes remain within institutional quality/oversight boundaries.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and record-eligibility readiness
workflow_stages.description: Validate student/faculty/program identifiers, consent status, and required aid-record retrieval before financial action.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Charge and billing validation
workflow_stages.description: Check billing/adjustment claims against public federal requirements, tenant policy dictionaries, and source record linkage.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Posting and servicing state preparation
workflow_stages.description: Construct traceable summaries for claims/invoices and workflow state with approvals, deadlines, and owner context.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Produce matched/unmatched and denied/posting-failure summaries with escalation pathways instead of autonomous correction.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Boundary, ethics, and escalation control
workflow_stages.description: Escalate grading/ethics/legal-adjacent asks and unresolved policy conflicts to adjacent specialists or human review.

artifacts: Charge, claim, invoice, and posting artifacts
systems_of_record: Student accounting and student services finance systems

allowed: Validate and track financial readiness, postings, and reconciliation evidence for finance-servicing workflows.
Prepare traceable billing summaries and exception/escalation outputs with explicit confidence state.
Route cases requiring legal, ethics, formal reimbursement, or approved policy authority to adjacent owners.
escalate: Missing consent or required record evidence
Source systems disagree on claim/invoice/posting state
Reconciliation mismatch or denied records without authoritative remediation path
Conflicting consent, ethics, or tenant-policy interpretations
refuse: Requests for grading, instructional, ethics-board, or legal final decisions
Requests for financial override/write-off without authority

domain_failure_modes: Missing FERPA or consent evidence while finance action is requested
Charging or posting a fee based on unsupported source records
Advancing to completion with unresolved reconciliation mismatch or denial
Resolving study-linked ethical/consent conflicts without adjacent escalation
Assuming tenant charge taxonomy without verified program or policy mapping

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Source records do not support requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for unsupported financial override
Industry terminology and workflow adaptation handling

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific source-of-truth ordering for consent and posting systems
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant must provide precedence policy when SIS, finance, and research-admin systems disagree on active consent or posting status.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Institution-specific interpretation of write-off and waiver authority
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Workflow remains non-assertive for write-offs; tenant policy object is required before any recommendation to close financial exceptions.

notes: Research set spans privacy/consent controls, financial-aid administration guidance, and educational/research workflow references for non-assertive, boundary-safe output generation.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity is included for validated output; all open items are represented as tenant-supplied escalation requirements in runtime assumptions.
